description: Validation rules and anti-patterns for HTTP / object-storage file upload endpoints. Combine magic-byte allowlist, filename sanitization, size/timeout limits, separate serving domain, and out-of-band virus scanning.

anti_patterns.rule: Trusting client-sent Content-Type for the allowlist check
Trusting the file extension for the allowlist check
Constructing storage path from user-supplied filename without sanitization (path traversal / Windows reserved names)
Serving uploads from the application origin makes HTML / SVG uploads same-origin XSS
No size limit at proxy and app allows trivial bandwidth + memory DoS
No virus scan turns the upload store into a malware distribution endpoint
SVG accepted without script / foreignObject / xlink:href stripping is a stored-XSS vector
ImageMagick with default policy.xml passes MVG / MSL / EPHEMERAL / HTTPS / URL delegates to shell — ImageTragick class
Extracting archives without canonicalizing entry paths against the destination directory permits zip-slip
S3 presigned upload URL without ContentType + key-prefix conditions lets attacker overwrite arbitrary keys
Accepting PDFs without pdfinfo structural validation permits XFA / JavaScript-in-PDF abuse
Not re-encoding images / videos / PDFs preserves polyglot and codec exploit payloads

magic_byte_signatures.notes: MZ — Windows PE; deny everywhere unless explicit
